classification_policy.secondary_theme_guideline: Assign secondary themes only when they are genuinely justified; many are allowed but sparse tagging is preferred.
classification_policy.confidence_definition: certainty that the label is correct, not card power level

cards.name: Abhorrent Overlord
cards.oracle_text:
Flying
When this creature enters, create a number of 1/1 black Harpy creature tokens with flying equal to your devotion to black. (Each {B} in the mana costs of permanents you control counts toward your devotion to black.)
At the beginning of your upkeep, sacrifice a creature.

themes.reasoning_notes: ETB token count scales directly with devotion to black, making it a clear devotion payoff rather than generic token support.
Creates multiple 1/1 flying Harpies on entry, so it meaningfully supports token plans, but only as a secondary theme here.
Its upkeep sacrifice clause and self-made expendable tokens naturally fit sacrifice shells as fodder support.

cards.name: Abzan Advantage
cards.oracle_text: Target player sacrifices an enchantment of their choice. Bolster 1. (Choose a creature with the least toughness among creatures you control and put a +1/+1 counter on it.)

themes.reasoning_notes: Instant forces an opponent to sacrifice an enchantment; this is clean enchantment interaction with minor upside.
Bolster 1 places a +1/+1 counter on your least-tough creature, giving small counters synergy support.

cards.name: Abzan Banner
cards.oracle_text:
{T}: Add {W}, {B}, or {G}.
{W}{B}{G}, {T}, Sacrifice this artifact: Draw a card.

themes.reasoning_notes: Three-mana artifact taps for W/B/G only; its defining deck role is Abzan color fixing from a rock slot.
Has a late-game cash-in mode, but paying WBG, tapping, and sacrificing for one card makes draw clearly secondary.

cards.name: Abzan Guide
cards.oracle_text:
Lifelink (Damage dealt by this creature also causes you to gain that much life.)
Morph {2}{W}{B}{G} (You may cast this card face down as a 2/2 creature for {3}. Turn it face up any time for its morph cost.)

themes.reasoning_notes: Lifelink makes it a repeatable life-gain source; mainly supports lifegain shells rather than paying them off.

cards.name: Abzan Runemark
cards.oracle_text:
Enchant creature
Enchanted creature gets +2/+2.
Enchanted creature has vigilance as long as you control a black or green permanent.

themes.reasoning_notes: Aura with a clean permanent buff; directly supports Aura-focused decks. Conditional vigilance is minor upside, not a separate theme.

cards.name: Abzan Skycaptain
cards.oracle_text:
Flying
When this creature dies, bolster 2. (Choose a creature with the least toughness among creatures you control and put two +1/+1 counters on it.)

themes.reasoning_notes: Bolster 2 always places two +1/+1 counters when it dies, making this a clean counters support card. It is not a dedicated payoff, and the body is too modest to justify extra themes.

cards.name: Accorder's Shield
cards.oracle_text:
Equipped creature gets +0/+3 and has vigilance. (Attacking doesn't cause it to tap.)
Equip {3} ({3}: Attach to target creature you control. Equip only as a sorcery.)

themes.reasoning_notes: Artifact Equipment with equip and a stat/keyword boost; fits equipment shells more than broader artifact themes.

cards.name: Accursed Spirit
cards.oracle_text: Intimidate (This creature can't be blocked except by artifact creatures and/or creatures that share a color with it.)

themes.reasoning_notes: Intimidate is a dedicated evasion keyword, making this mainly an evasive attacker rather than a true midrange payoff or finisher.

cards.name: Acolyte's Reward
cards.oracle_text: Prevent the next X damage that would be dealt to target creature this turn, where X is your devotion to white. If damage is prevented this way, Acolyte's Reward deals that much damage to any target. (Each {W} in the mana costs of permanents you control counts toward your devotion to white.)

themes.reasoning_notes: Instant-speed prevention can win combat, and prevented damage is redirected to any target for a reactive blowout.
Its prevention and damage output scale directly with your devotion to white, making it a modest devotion payoff.
If enough damage is prevented, the reflected damage can kill a creature, but only in reactive damage-based spots.

cards.name: Act of Treason
cards.oracle_text: Gain control of target creature until end of turn. Untap that creature. It gains haste until end of turn. (It can attack and {T} this turn.)

themes.reasoning_notes: Temporary steal plus untap/haste creates a swingy combat turn and short-term board advantage.
Threaten effect is a classic fodder-stealing setup for sacrifice decks to permanently convert the borrowed creature.

cards.name: Adaptive Snapjaw
cards.oracle_text: Evolve (Whenever a creature you control enters, if that creature has greater power or toughness than this creature, put a +1/+1 counter on this creature.)

themes.reasoning_notes: Evolve repeatedly places +1/+1 counters on itself when larger creatures enter, making it a clear counters-synergy piece.

cards.name: Aegis Angel
cards.oracle_text:
Flying (This creature can't be blocked except by creatures with flying or reach.)
When this creature enters, another target permanent gains indestructible for as long as you control this creature. (Effects that say "destroy" don't destroy it. A creature with indestructible can't be destroyed by damage.)

themes.reasoning_notes: Six-mana 5/5 flyer is a classic top-end board stabilizer, and its ETB can protect another key permanent with conditional indestructible.
Flying gives it real evasive pressure, but the card does not specifically reward or care about other fliers.

cards.name: Aerial Formation
cards.oracle_text:
Strive — This spell costs {2}{U} more to cast for each target beyond the first.
Any number of target creatures each get +1/+1 and gain flying until end of turn.

themes.reasoning_notes: Cheap instant-speed pump plus flying for combat; strive can scale it across multiple attackers or blockers.
It targets your creatures, so it can trigger heroic, but that is incidental rather than a dedicated payoff.

cards.name: Aerie Bowmasters
cards.oracle_text:
Reach (This creature can block creatures with flying.)
Megamorph {5}{G} (You may cast this card face down as a 2/2 creature for {3}. Turn it face up any time for its megamorph cost and put a +1/+1 counter on it.)

themes.reasoning_notes: Megamorph is the card's defining mechanic; it supports face-down/morph synergies more than counter synergies.
Reach specifically interacts with flyers, giving it a small anti-flying role in combat.

cards.name: Aeronaut Cavalry
cards.oracle_text:
Flying
When this creature enters, put a +1/+1 counter on another target Soldier you control.

themes.reasoning_notes: Its ETB specifically rewards controlling another Soldier, making it a clear Soldier-tribal payoff rather than a generic flyer.

cards.name: Aeronaut Tinkerer
cards.oracle_text: This creature has flying as long as you control an artifact. (It can't be blocked except by creatures with flying or reach.)

themes.reasoning_notes: Conditional flying turns artifact presence into a meaningful combat reward, so it fits artifact decks as a modest payoff creature.
Its only extra functionality is gaining flying, making evasive pressure a real but clearly secondary theme.

cards.name: Aether Figment
cards.oracle_text:
Kicker {3} (You may pay an additional {3} as you cast this spell.)
If this creature was kicked, it enters with two +1/+1 counters on it.
This creature can't be blocked.

themes.reasoning_notes: Oracle text makes it unblockable outright; that is the card's most defining deckbuilding hook, more than kicker counters.
Kicker adds two +1/+1 counters, but only as a self-contained upgrade rather than a dedicated counters payoff.

cards.name: Aetherplasm
cards.oracle_text: Whenever this creature blocks a creature, you may return this creature to its owner's hand. If you do, you may put a creature card from your hand onto the battlefield blocking that creature.

themes.reasoning_notes: Its trigger only works when it blocks, so the card is fundamentally built around defensive combat positioning.
Can convert a block into a surprise blocker from hand, creating a combat swing, but this is narrower than a general tempo card.

cards.name: Affa Guard Hound
cards.oracle_text:
Flash (You may cast this spell any time you could cast an instant.)
When this creature enters, target creature gets +0/+3 until end of turn.

themes.reasoning_notes: Flash plus ETB +0/+3 creates an instant-speed combat blowout or save, fitting defensive combat-trick play patterns.

cards.name: Agility
cards.oracle_text:
Enchant creature
Enchanted creature gets +1/+1 and has flanking. (Whenever a creature without flanking blocks this creature, the blocking creature gets -1/-1 until end of turn.)

themes.reasoning_notes: Cheap Aura that directly enchants a creature and grants a permanent +1/+1 combat upgrade.
Flanking mainly helps attackers push through blocks, so it fits aggressive combat support more than a true combat trick.

cards.name: Agoraphobia
cards.oracle_text:
Enchant creature
Enchanted creature gets -5/-0.
{2}{U}: Return this Aura to its owner's hand.

themes.reasoning_notes: Aura-based creature debuff; recurring bounce mode lets it be reused on new targets.
-5/-0 suppresses attackers efficiently, and self-bounce supports repeated tempo-positive redeployment.

cards.name: Ainok Bond-Kin
cards.oracle_text:
Outlast {1}{W} ({1}{W}, {T}: Put a +1/+1 counter on this creature. Outlast only as a sorcery.)
Each creature you control with a +1/+1 counter on it has first strike.

themes.reasoning_notes: Outlast places a +1/+1 counter, and it explicitly rewards countered creatures by granting them first strike.

cards.name: Ainok Guide
cards.oracle_text:
When this creature enters, choose one —
• Put a +1/+1 counter on this creature.
• Search your library for a basic land card, reveal it, then shuffle and put that card on top.

themes.reasoning_notes: Can tutor any basic land to the top, smoothing colors and land drops, but it does not put the land onto the battlefield.
One ETB mode puts a +1/+1 counter on itself, giving it a small but real counters hook.

cards.name: Ajani's Pridemate
cards.oracle_text: Whenever you gain life, put a +1/+1 counter on this creature.

themes.reasoning_notes: Directly rewards every life-gain event by growing, making it a textbook lifegain payoff creature.
Uses +1/+1 counters as its growth mechanism, but only on itself rather than broadly enabling counters strategies.

cards.name: Akoum Flameseeker
cards.oracle_text: Cohort — {T}, Tap an untapped Ally you control: Discard a card. If you do, draw a card.

themes.reasoning_notes: Cohort requires tapping another Ally, so it functions mainly as an Ally-tribal synergy piece rather than a standalone rummager.
It discards as part of a rummage ability, giving light discard-synergy support, but that theme is clearly secondary.

cards.name: Akroan Crusader
cards.oracle_text: Heroic — Whenever you cast a spell that targets this creature, create a 1/1 red Soldier creature token with haste. (It can attack and {T} as soon as it comes under your control.)

themes.reasoning_notes: Heroic directly rewards targeting it with spells by generating board presence, making it a clear heroic payoff.
Repeated targeted spells convert into 1/1 haste Soldier tokens, giving it a real token-production engine role.

cards.name: Akroan Hoplite
cards.oracle_text: Whenever this creature attacks, it gets +X/+0 until end of turn, where X is the number of attacking creatures you control.

themes.reasoning_notes: Attack trigger scales with your total attacking creatures, directly rewarding wide combat boards and swarm attacks.

cards.name: Akroan Horse
cards.oracle_text:
Defender
When this creature enters, an opponent gains control of it.
At the beginning of your upkeep, each opponent creates a 1/1 white Soldier creature token.

themes.reasoning_notes: Artifact creature with no artifact-matters text; artifact shells can play it, but its Oracle text does not reward artifact synergies directly.
It repeatedly creates Soldier tokens, but for opponents on your upkeep, so this is token production without typical token-deck payoff alignment.

cards.name: Akroan Mastiff
cards.oracle_text: {W}, {T}: Tap target creature.

themes.reasoning_notes: Repeatable tap ability can suppress opposing creatures over time, fitting slow creature-based board control more than any narrower theme.

cards.name: Akroan Phalanx
cards.oracle_text:
Vigilance
{2}{R}: Creatures you control get +1/+0 until end of turn.

themes.reasoning_notes: Repeatable team-wide power boost rewards a broad board of creatures; vigilance helps it attack or hold back.

cards.name: Akroan Skyguard
cards.oracle_text:
Flying
Heroic — Whenever you cast a spell that targets this creature, put a +1/+1 counter on this creature.

themes.reasoning_notes: Heroic explicitly rewards targeting it with spells by growing via +1/+1 counters.
Its heroic trigger places +1/+1 counters on itself as the scaling mechanism.

cards.name: Alesha, Who Smiles at Death
cards.oracle_text:
First strike
Whenever Alesha attacks, you may pay {W/B}{W/B}. If you do, return target creature card with power 2 or less from your graveyard to the battlefield tapped and attacking.

themes.reasoning_notes: Attack trigger reanimates a power-2-or-less creature from your graveyard directly to the battlefield tapped and attacking.

cards.name: Alesha's Vanguard
cards.oracle_text: Dash {2}{B} (You may cast this spell for its dash cost. If you do, it gains haste, and it's returned from the battlefield to its owner's hand at the beginning of the next end step.)

themes.reasoning_notes: Dash enables hasty, mana-efficient attacks while self-bouncing to dodge sorcery-speed removal; the card mainly plays as a minor tempo tool.

cards.name: Ambush Paratrooper
cards.oracle_text:
Flash
Flying
{5}: Creatures you control get +1/+1 until end of turn.

themes.reasoning_notes: Repeatable team pump rewards having multiple creatures; effect scales with a wide board more than with tokens specifically.
The {5} activation gives late-game utility, letting excess mana convert into incremental combat pressure.

cards.name: Anax and Cymede
cards.oracle_text:
First strike, vigilance
Heroic — Whenever you cast a spell that targets Anax and Cymede, creatures you control get +1/+1 and gain trample until end of turn.

themes.reasoning_notes: Has Heroic and rewards targeted spells with a team-wide +1/+1 and trample alpha-strike effect.

cards.name: Ancestral Vengeance
cards.oracle_text:
Enchant creature
When this Aura enters, put a +1/+1 counter on target creature you control.
Enchanted creature gets -1/-1.

themes.reasoning_notes: Type line is Aura and it has enchant creature; its clearest deckbuilding home is aura-centric shells.
ETB puts a +1/+1 counter on a creature you control, but that rider is incidental rather than a dedicated counters payoff.

cards.name: Ancient Silverback
cards.oracle_text: {G}: Regenerate this creature. (The next time this creature would be destroyed this turn, instead tap it, remove it from combat, and heal all damage on it.)

themes.reasoning_notes: Large 6/5 body with self-regeneration; mainly a resilient curve-top creature for creature-based midrange decks.

cards.name: Angel of Renewal
cards.oracle_text:
Flying
When this creature enters, you gain 1 life for each creature you control.

themes.reasoning_notes: ETB gains life equal to creatures you control, making it a clear lifegain enabler on a single card.
The life-gain trigger scales with total creature count, so it lightly rewards creature-dense go-wide boards.

cards.name: Angelic Benediction
cards.oracle_text:
Exalted (Whenever a creature you control attacks alone, that creature gets +1/+1 until end of turn.)
Whenever a creature you control attacks alone, you may tap target creature.

themes.reasoning_notes: Exalted and the attack-alone tap trigger both reward committing to one attacker, a classic single-creature combat plan.
Tapping a potential blocker each turn creates soft combat tempo, but only within the attack-alone shell rather than as a general tempo card.

cards.name: Angelic Gift
cards.oracle_text:
Enchant creature
When this Aura enters, draw a card.
Enchanted creature has flying.

themes.reasoning_notes: Cheap Aura that targets a creature and adds an on-theme body upgrade; type line and oracle text make this a clear Auras enabler.
It replaces itself when it enters, so the draw is real but incidental rather than a dedicated card-draw engine.

cards.name: Angelic Wall
cards.oracle_text:
Defender (This creature can't attack.)
Flying

themes.reasoning_notes: Cheap flying defender blocks early attackers well; a pure defensive body that supports control/stall plans.

cards.name: Anthousa, Setessan Hero
cards.oracle_text: Heroic — Whenever you cast a spell that targets Anthousa, up to three target lands you control each become 2/2 Warrior creatures until end of turn. They're still lands.

themes.reasoning_notes: Has Heroic and directly rewards targeting it with spells by animating up to three lands into attackers or blockers.

cards.name: Anticipate
cards.oracle_text: Look at the top three cards of your library. Put one of them into your hand and the rest on the bottom of your library in any order.

themes.reasoning_notes: Cheap instant-speed selection replaces itself by taking 1 of top 3, fitting card draw/card selection support.
A low-cost instant is a reasonable spell-count enabler, but it has no explicit spell-matter payoff text.

cards.name: Anvilwrought Raptor
cards.oracle_text:
Flying
First strike (This creature deals combat damage before creatures without first strike.)

themes.reasoning_notes: Artifact creature with no broader synergy text; it mainly counts for artifact-matters decks while serving as an evasive body.

cards.name: Aqueous Form
cards.oracle_text:
Enchant creature
Enchanted creature can't be blocked.
Whenever enchanted creature attacks, scry 1. (Look at the top card of your library. You may put that card on the bottom.)

themes.reasoning_notes: One-mana Aura with a strong creature enhancement; directly fits aura-focused decks and is efficient glue for enchant-creature plans.
Permanent unblockable plus repeatable attack-trigger scry strongly supports suit-up-one-creature Voltron gameplans more than generic tempo.

cards.name: Arachnus Web
cards.oracle_text:
Enchant creature
Enchanted creature can't attack or block, and its activated abilities can't be activated.
At the beginning of the end step, if enchanted creature's power is 4 or greater, destroy this Aura.

themes.reasoning_notes: Aura-based creature lockdown; type line is Aura and oracle text fully disables combat and activated abilities.
Single-target lockdown piece that suppresses a creature until the Aura breaks on power 4 or greater.

cards.name: Aragorn and Arwen, Wed
cards.oracle_text:
Vigilance
Whenever Aragorn and Arwen enters or attacks, put a +1/+1 counter on each other creature you control. You gain 1 life for each other creature you control.

inventory.set_name: The Lord of the Rings: Tales of Middle-earth

themes.reasoning_notes: Its ETB/attack trigger puts a +1/+1 counter on each other creature you control, making counters the card's clear build-around role. The life gain scales wide but is secondary, not a distinct theme anchor.

cards.name: Arashin Cleric
cards.oracle_text: When this creature enters, you gain 3 life.

themes.reasoning_notes: ETB gains 3 life on a cheap body, mainly serving as a simple lifegain enabler for payoff-focused decks.

cards.name: Archers of Qarsi
cards.oracle_text:
Defender
Reach (This creature can block creatures with flying.)

themes.reasoning_notes: Defender makes it a dedicated blocker, and reach specifically improves its role at holding off aerial attacks.

cards.name: Archon of the Triumvirate
cards.oracle_text:
Flying
Whenever this creature attacks, detain up to two target nonland permanents your opponents control. (Until your next turn, those permanents can't attack or block and their activated abilities can't be activated.)

themes.reasoning_notes: Attack-triggered detain is temporary board suppression, creating swing turns rather than permanent control; flying body helps convert that into pressure.
Repeatable detain can support control shells, but its attack-gated, until-next-turn effect is more tempo-oriented than classic control.

cards.name: Arctic Flats
cards.oracle_text:
This land enters tapped.
{T}: Add {G} or {W}.

themes.reasoning_notes: Tapped dual land that provides both {G} and {W}; its main deckbuilding role is color fixing.

cards.name: Argothian Opportunist
cards.oracle_text: When this creature enters, create a tapped Powerstone token. (It's an artifact with "{T}: Add {C}. This mana can't be spent to cast a nonartifact spell.")

themes.reasoning_notes: ETB makes a Powerstone token, providing repeatable mana acceleration for artifact-heavy or top-end decks.
Creates an artifact token on entry, giving incidental artifact count and artifact-token support.

cards.name: Arm with Aether
cards.oracle_text: Until end of turn, creatures you control gain "Whenever this creature deals damage to an opponent, you may return target creature that player controls to its owner's hand."

themes.reasoning_notes: Turns combat damage into temporary creature removal, pushing attacks through and setting opponents back on board and mana.
Works best when several creatures can connect, since each damage trigger can bounce another opposing creature.

cards.name: Armament of Nyx
cards.oracle_text:
Enchant creature
Enchanted creature has double strike as long as it's an enchantment. Otherwise, prevent all damage that would be dealt by enchanted creature. (A creature with double strike deals both first-strike and regular combat damage.)

themes.reasoning_notes: Aura by type, and its best-case use is suiting up an enchantment creature with double strike; strongest home is Aura-centric decks.
Double strike is a classic single-creature payoff, though here it only turns on when the enchanted creature is itself an enchantment.

cards.name: Armed // Dangerous
cards.oracle_text:
Armed: Target creature gets +1/+1 and gains double strike until end of turn.
Fuse (You may cast one or both halves of this card from your hand.) // Dangerous: All creatures able to block target creature this turn do so.
Fuse (You may cast one or both halves of this card from your hand.)

themes.reasoning_notes: Both halves are combat-focused targeted effects: pump plus double strike, or forced blocks to engineer a favorable attack. Heroic is only incidental, not a real theme tag here.

cards.name: Armored Transport
cards.oracle_text: Prevent all combat damage that would be dealt to this creature by creatures blocking it.

themes.reasoning_notes: Colorless artifact creature with no stronger archetype hook; it mainly contributes as an artifact-body for artifact synergies.
Only meaningful deckbuilding hook is the Horse creature type; blank oracle text and vanilla stats do not point to another theme.

cards.name: Armory of Iroas
cards.oracle_text:
Whenever equipped creature attacks, put a +1/+1 counter on it.
Equip {2} ({2}: Attach to target creature you control. Equip only as a sorcery.)

themes.reasoning_notes: Artifact Equipment with equip; its core deckbuilding home is any equipment shell that repeatedly buffs one attacker.
Attack trigger adds a +1/+1 counter each combat, so it supports counters plans but is not a dedicated counters payoff.

cards.name: Arrow Volley Trap
cards.oracle_text:
If four or more creatures are attacking, you may pay {1}{W} rather than pay this spell's mana cost.
Arrow Volley Trap deals 5 damage divided as you choose among any number of target attacking creatures.

themes.reasoning_notes: Divided damage to target attacking creatures is reactive creature removal, especially against combat or wide attacks.

cards.name: Arrows of Justice
cards.oracle_text: Arrows of Justice deals 4 damage to target attacking or blocking creature.

themes.reasoning_notes: Deals 4 damage to one target creature, but only if it is attacking or blocking; this is conditional combat removal.

cards.name: Artful Maneuver
cards.oracle_text:
Target creature gets +2/+2 until end of turn.
Rebound (If you cast this spell from your hand, exile it as it resolves. At the beginning of your next upkeep, you may cast this card from exile without paying its mana cost.)

themes.reasoning_notes: Instant +2/+2 pump is a classic combat trick; rebound lets it swing combat twice across two turns.
Targets your creature, and rebound can retrigger heroic-style payoffs on the next upkeep.

cards.name: Artisan of Forms
cards.oracle_text: Heroic — Whenever you cast a spell that targets this creature, you may have this creature become a copy of target creature, except it has this ability.

themes.reasoning_notes: Heroic is explicit in oracle text; it directly rewards targeting your own creature and preserves the ability through copying.
Copying the best creature for low cost can create a temporary efficiency swing, but tempo is only loosely implied, not a core build-around.

cards.name: Artisan's Sorrow
cards.oracle_text: Destroy target artifact or enchantment. Scry 2. (Look at the top two cards of your library, then put any number of them on the bottom and the rest on top in any order.)

themes.reasoning_notes: Instant-speed artifact/enchantment removal; scry 2 adds small selection but the card's core role is targeted interaction.

cards.name: Aspect of Gorgon
cards.oracle_text:
Enchant creature
Enchanted creature gets +1/+3 and has deathtouch. (Any amount of damage it deals to a creature is enough to destroy it.)

themes.reasoning_notes: Aura enchantment that buffs a creature with +1/+3 and deathtouch; fits Aura-based decks first.
Granting deathtouch makes blocks and attacks highly favorable, functioning as repeatable combat-based interaction.

cards.name: Aspect of Hydra
cards.oracle_text: Target creature gets +X/+X until end of turn, where X is your devotion to green. (Each {G} in the mana costs of permanents you control counts toward your devotion to green.)

themes.reasoning_notes: Scales directly with your devotion to green, making it a dedicated reward for heavy green-pip board states.
Cheap instant-speed pump can swing combat or push lethal, but its ceiling depends on devotion support.

cards.name: Asphodel Wanderer
cards.oracle_text: {2}{B}: Regenerate this creature.

themes.reasoning_notes: A 1/1 for {B} with repeatable regeneration mainly plays as a sticky blocker, not a real midrange payoff or broader synergy piece.

cards.name: Asphyxiate
cards.oracle_text: Destroy target untapped creature.

themes.reasoning_notes: Sorcery that directly destroys a target creature; a straightforward removal spell with an untapped restriction.

cards.name: Assassin's Strike
cards.oracle_text: Destroy target creature. Its controller discards a card.

themes.reasoning_notes: Sorcery-speed creature kill; primary gameplay function is single-target removal.
Also makes that creature's controller discard a card, but this is a secondary rider.

cards.name: Assault Griffin
cards.oracle_text: Flying

themes.reasoning_notes: Has only flying on a 3/2 body; it cleanly fits generic fliers support more than a nonsynergy midrange theme.

cards.name: Atarka Efreet
cards.oracle_text:
Megamorph {2}{R} (You may cast this card face down as a 2/2 creature for {3}. Turn it face up any time for its megamorph cost and put a +1/+1 counter on it.)
When this creature is turned face up, it deals 1 damage to any target.

themes.reasoning_notes: Megamorph is the card's core mechanic and primary deckbuilding hook; the face-up trigger specifically rewards morph play patterns.
Its turned-face-up trigger can ping any target, giving it minor removal or utility interaction in morph shells.

cards.name: Auramancer
cards.oracle_text: When this creature enters, you may return target enchantment card from your graveyard to your hand.

themes.reasoning_notes: ETB returns an enchantment from graveyard to hand, a classic enchantment/aura recursion support piece.
Directly recovers an enchantment card from your graveyard, but only to hand and only for enchantments.

cards.name: Aven Skirmisher
cards.oracle_text: Flying

themes.reasoning_notes: Cheap creature with only flying; clean support piece for flying/evasion decks, not a broader payoff or engine.

cards.name: Aven Surveyor
cards.oracle_text:
Flying
When this creature enters, choose one —
• Put a +1/+1 counter on this creature.
• Return target creature to its owner's hand.

themes.reasoning_notes: Its strongest mode is an ETB creature bounce, generating board-position tempo while leaving behind a flier.
It can enter with a +1/+1 counter, but that mode is incidental and not a real counters payoff or engine.

cards.name: Awaken the Bear
cards.oracle_text: Target creature gets +3/+3 and gains trample until end of turn.

themes.reasoning_notes: Instant grants +3/+3 and trample to a creature for one turn; this is a classic pump spell used to win combat or push damage.

cards.name: Azor's Elocutors
cards.oracle_text:
At the beginning of your upkeep, put a filibuster counter on this creature. Then if this creature has five or more filibuster counters on it, you win the game.
Whenever a source deals damage to you, remove a filibuster counter from this creature.

themes.reasoning_notes: Alternate win condition that specifically rewards preventing damage to you over several turns, a clear fit for control or pillow-fort shells.

cards.name: Back to Nature
cards.oracle_text: Destroy all enchantments.

themes.reasoning_notes: Cheap instant-speed sweeper for enchantments; mainly a sideboard-style answer that disrupts enchantment-based boards.

cards.name: Bag End Porter
cards.oracle_text: Whenever this creature attacks, it gets +X/+X until end of turn, where X is the number of legendary creatures you control.

inventory.set_name: The Lord of the Rings: Tales of Middle-earth

themes.reasoning_notes: Attack trigger scales directly with the number of legendary creatures you control, making it a straightforward legends-matter payoff.

cards.name: Baleful Eidolon
cards.oracle_text:
Bestow {4}{B} (If you cast this card for its bestow cost, it's an Aura spell with enchant creature. It becomes a creature again if it's not attached.)
Deathtouch (Any amount of damage this deals to a creature is enough to destroy it.)
Enchanted creature gets +1/+1 and has deathtouch.

themes.reasoning_notes: Bestow turns it into an Aura that buffs a creature and grants deathtouch; its main deckbuilding home is Aura-based enchantment shells.

cards.name: Baloth Pup
cards.oracle_text: This creature has trample as long as it has a +1/+1 counter on it.

cards.name: Executioner's Swing

Flying

Flying

Flying

cards.name: Fall of the Hammer

cards.name: Zhur-Taa Swine

themes.reasoning_notes: Bloodrush functions as an instant-speed pump effect, giving an attacker +5/+4 for a surprise combat swing.
5/4 body for 5 and Bloodrush only buffs an attacking creature, pointing clearly to aggressive combat plans.

cards.name: Zof Shade
cards.oracle_text: {2}{B}: This creature gets +2/+2 until end of turn.

themes.reasoning_notes: Repeatable pump ability turns excess mana into combat stats; classic Shade-style mana sink for closing games.

cards.name: Zulaport Chainmage
cards.oracle_text: Cohort — {T}, Tap an untapped Ally you control: Target opponent loses 2 life.

themes.reasoning_notes: Cohort specifically requires another Ally, making it an Ally-tribal card that rewards tribal board presence with repeatable life loss.
